result.stdout:
Sleet 7.1.0

Usage: sleet [command] [options]

Options:
  -h|--help      Show help information.
  --version      Show version information.

Commands:
  createconfig   Create a new sleet.json config file.
  delete         Delete a package or packages from a feed.
  destroy        Delete all files from a feed.
  download       Download packages from a feed to a local folder.
  feed-settings  Read or modify feed settings stored in sleet.settings.json for
                 the feed.
  init           Initialize a new sleet feed.
  push           Push a package to a feed.
  recreate       Recreate a feed. This downloads all packages, deletes the feed,
                 and then creates a new feed from the existing packages. This
                 may be used to fix feed problems or to upgrade between Sleet
                 versions.
  retention      Package retention commands for pruning and limiting package
                 versions.
  stats          Report feed statistics.
  validate       Validate a feed.

Run 'sleet [command] -h|--help' for more information about a command.

commands.payload:
Sleet 7.1.0

Usage: sleet [command] [options]

Options:
  -h|--help      Show help information.
  --version      Show version information.

Commands:
  createconfig   Create a new sleet.json config file.
  delete         Delete a package or packages from a feed.
  destroy        Delete all files from a feed.
  download       Download packages from a feed to a local folder.
  feed-settings  Read or modify feed settings stored in sleet.settings.json for
                 the feed.
  init           Initialize a new sleet feed.
  push           Push a package to a feed.
  recreate       Recreate a feed. This downloads all packages, deletes the feed,
                 and then creates a new feed from the existing packages. This
                 may be used to fix feed problems or to upgrade between Sleet
                 versions.
  retention      Package retention commands for pruning and limiting package
                 versions.
  stats          Report feed statistics.
  validate       Validate a feed.

Run 'sleet [command] -h|--help' for more information about a command.

result.stdout:
Create a new sleet.json config file.

Usage: sleet createconfig [options]

Options:
  --s3       Add a template entry for an Amazon S3 storage feed.
  --azure    Add a template entry for an azure storage feed.
  --local    Add a template entry for a local folder feed.
  --output   Output path. If not specified the file will be created in the
             working directory.
  --verbose  Write out additional messages for verbose output.
  -h|--help  Show help information.

commands.payload:
Create a new sleet.json config file.

Usage: sleet createconfig [options]

Options:
  --s3       Add a template entry for an Amazon S3 storage feed.
  --azure    Add a template entry for an azure storage feed.
  --local    Add a template entry for a local folder feed.
  --output   Output path. If not specified the file will be created in the
             working directory.
  --verbose  Write out additional messages for verbose output.
  -h|--help  Show help information.

result.stdout:
Delete a package or packages from a feed.

Usage: sleet delete [options]

Options:
  -c|--config    sleet.json file to read sources and settings from. Specify
                 'none' to avoid loading.
  -s|--source    Source name from sleet.json.
  -i|--id        Package id.
  -v|--version   Package version. If this is not specified all versions will be
                 deleted.
  -r|--reason    Reason for deleting the package.
  -f|--force     Ignore missing packages.
  --verbose      Write out additional messages for verbose output.
  -p|--property  Define a key=value pair. Used for tokenized sleet.json files or
                 env var values.
  -h|--help      Show help information.

result.stdout:
Download packages from a feed to a local folder.

Usage: sleet download [options]

Options:
  -c|--config       sleet.json file to read sources and settings from. Specify
                    'none' to avoid loading.
  -s|--source       Source name from sleet.json.
  --verbose         Write out additional messages for verbose output.
  -p|--property     Define a key=value pair. Used for tokenized sleet.json files
                    or env var values.
  -h|--help         Show help information.
  -o|--output-path  Output directory to store downloaded nupkgs.
  --skip-existing   Skip packages that already exist in the output folder.
  --no-lock         Skip locking the feed and verifying the client version.
  --ignore-errors   Ignore download errors.

commands.payload:
Download packages from a feed to a local folder.

Usage: sleet download [options]

Options:
  -c|--config       sleet.json file to read sources and settings from. Specify
                    'none' to avoid loading.
  -s|--source       Source name from sleet.json.
  --verbose         Write out additional messages for verbose output.
  -p|--property     Define a key=value pair. Used for tokenized sleet.json files
                    or env var values.
  -h|--help         Show help information.
  -o|--output-path  Output directory to store downloaded nupkgs.
  --skip-existing   Skip packages that already exist in the output folder.
  --no-lock         Skip locking the feed and verifying the client version.
  --ignore-errors   Ignore download errors.

result.stdout:
Read or modify feed settings stored in sleet.settings.json for the feed.

Usage: sleet feed-settings [options]

Options:
  -c|--config    sleet.json file to read sources and settings from. Specify
                 'none' to avoid loading.
  -s|--source    Source name from sleet.json.
  --verbose      Write out additional messages for verbose output.
  -h|--help      Show help information.
  --unset-all    Clear all feed settings.
  --unset        Remove a feed setting. May be specified multiple times.
  --set          Add a feed setting. Value must be in the form {key}:{value} May
                 be specified multiple times.
  --get          Display a feed setting. May be specified multiple times.
  --get-all      Diplay all feed settings.
  -p|--property  Define a key=value pair. Used for tokenized sleet.json files or
                 env var values.

commands.payload:
Read or modify feed settings stored in sleet.settings.json for the feed.

Usage: sleet feed-settings [options]

Options:
  -c|--config    sleet.json file to read sources and settings from. Specify
                 'none' to avoid loading.
  -s|--source    Source name from sleet.json.
  --verbose      Write out additional messages for verbose output.
  -h|--help      Show help information.
  --unset-all    Clear all feed settings.
  --unset        Remove a feed setting. May be specified multiple times.
  --set          Add a feed setting. Value must be in the form {key}:{value} May
                 be specified multiple times.
  --get          Display a feed setting. May be specified multiple times.
  --get-all      Diplay all feed settings.
  -p|--property  Define a key=value pair. Used for tokenized sleet.json files or
                 env var values.

result.stdout:
Initialize a new sleet feed.

Usage: sleet init [options]

Options:
  -c|--config     sleet.json file to read sources and settings from. Specify
                  'none' to avoid loading.
  -s|--source     Source name from sleet.json.
  --verbose       Write out additional messages for verbose output.
  -h|--help       Show help information.
  --with-catalog  Enable the feed catalog and all change history tracking.
  --with-symbols  Enable symbols server.
  -p|--property   Define a key=value pair. Used for tokenized sleet.json files
                  or env var values.

result.stdout:
Push a package to a feed.

Usage: sleet push [options] <[root]>

Arguments:
  [root]           Paths to individual packages or directories containing
                   packages.

Options:
  -c|--config      sleet.json file to read sources and settings from. Specify
                   'none' to avoid loading.
  -s|--source      Source name from sleet.json.
  --verbose        Write out additional messages for verbose output.
  -f|--force       Overwrite existing packages.
  --skip-existing  Skip packages that already exist on the feed.
  -p|--property    Define a key=value pair. Used for tokenized sleet.json files
                   or env var values.
  -h|--help        Show help information.

commands.payload:
Push a package to a feed.

Usage: sleet push [options] <[root]>

Arguments:
  [root]           Paths to individual packages or directories containing
                   packages.

Options:
  -c|--config      sleet.json file to read sources and settings from. Specify
                   'none' to avoid loading.
  -s|--source      Source name from sleet.json.
  --verbose        Write out additional messages for verbose output.
  -f|--force       Overwrite existing packages.
  --skip-existing  Skip packages that already exist on the feed.
  -p|--property    Define a key=value pair. Used for tokenized sleet.json files
                   or env var values.
  -h|--help        Show help information.

result.stdout:
Recreate a feed. This downloads all packages, deletes the feed, and then creates a new feed from the existing packages. This may be used to fix feed problems or to upgrade between Sleet versions.

Usage: sleet recreate [options]

Options:
  -c|--config    sleet.json file to read sources and settings from. Specify
                 'none' to avoid loading.
  -s|--source    Source name from sleet.json.
  --verbose      Write out additional messages for verbose output.
  -h|--help      Show help information.
  --nupkg-path   Optional temporary directory to store downloaded nupkgs in.
                 This folder will be cleaned up if the command completes
                 successfully. If the command fails these files will be left as
                 a backup.
  -f|--force     Ignore errors when recreating the feed.
  -p|--property  Define a key=value pair. Used for tokenized sleet.json files or
                 env var values.

commands.payload:
Recreate a feed. This downloads all packages, deletes the feed, and then creates a new feed from the existing packages. This may be used to fix feed problems or to upgrade between Sleet versions.

  -s|--source    Source name from sleet.json.
  --verbose      Write out additional messages for verbose output.
  -h|--help      Show help information.
  --nupkg-path   Optional temporary directory to store downloaded nupkgs in.
                 This folder will be cleaned up if the command completes
                 successfully. If the command fails these files will be left as
                 a backup.
  -f|--force     Ignore errors when recreating the feed.
  -p|--property  Define a key=value pair. Used for tokenized sleet.json files or
                 env var values.

result.stdout:
Package retention commands for pruning and limiting package versions.

Usage: sleet retention [command] [options]

Options:
  -h|--help  Show help information.

Commands:
  prune      Prune feed packages.
  settings   Modify feed settings to automatically prune packages on push.

Run 'retention [command] -h|--help' for more information about a command.

commands.payload:
Package retention commands for pruning and limiting package versions.

Usage: sleet retention [command] [options]

Options:
  -h|--help  Show help information.

Commands:
  prune      Prune feed packages.
  settings   Modify feed settings to automatically prune packages on push.

Run 'retention [command] -h|--help' for more information about a command.

result.stdout:
Prune feed packages.

Usage: sleet retention prune [options]

Options:
  -c|--config       sleet.json file to read sources and settings from. Specify
                    'none' to avoid loading.
  -s|--source       Source name from sleet.json.
  --verbose         Write out additional messages for verbose output.
  -h|--help         Show help information.
  --stable          Number of stable versions per package id. If not specified
                    the feed settings will be used.
  --prerelease      Number of prerelease versions per package id. If not
                    specified the feed settings will be used.
  --release-labels  Group prerelease packages by the first X release labels.
                    Each group will be pruned to the prerelease max if applied.
                    (optional)
  --package         Prune only the given package ids
  --dry-run         Print out all versions that would be deleted without
                    actually removing them.
  -p|--property     Define a key=value pair. Used for tokenized sleet.json files
                    or env var values.

commands.payload:
Prune feed packages.

Usage: sleet retention prune [options]

Options:
  -c|--config       sleet.json file to read sources and settings from. Specify
                    'none' to avoid loading.
  -s|--source       Source name from sleet.json.
  --verbose         Write out additional messages for verbose output.
  -h|--help         Show help information.
  --stable          Number of stable versions per package id. If not specified
                    the feed settings will be used.
  --prerelease      Number of prerelease versions per package id. If not
                    specified the feed settings will be used.
  --release-labels  Group prerelease packages by the first X release labels.
                    Each group will be pruned to the prerelease max if applied.
                    (optional)
  --package         Prune only the given package ids
  --dry-run         Print out all versions that would be deleted without
                    actually removing them.
  -p|--property     Define a key=value pair. Used for tokenized sleet.json files
                    or env var values.

result.stdout:
Modify feed settings to automatically prune packages on push.

Usage: sleet retention settings [options]

Options:
  -c|--config       sleet.json file to read sources and settings from. Specify
                    'none' to avoid loading.
  -s|--source       Source name from sleet.json.
  --verbose         Write out additional messages for verbose output.
  -h|--help         Show help information.
  --stable          Number of stable versions per package id to retain.
  --prerelease      Number of prerelease versions per package id to retain.
  --release-labels  Group prerelease packages by the first X release labels.
                    Each group will be pruned to the prerelease max if applied.
                    (optional)
  --disable         Disable package retention.
  -p|--property     Define a key=value pair. Used for tokenized sleet.json files
                    or env var values.
